proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous predictor feature_080 (range 30-90, mean 65) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_080 corresponds to longer progression-free survival.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df['feature_080'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.7015, p < 1e-300. Univariate OLS slope ~ +0.18 months PFS per unit feature_080. Quintile means: Q1=2.28, Q5=7.21 months. By far the strongest single predictor of PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 3-level ordinal feature feature_063 (levels 0, 1, 2; resembling a stage variable) shows a monotonic negative trend with pfs_months: each higher level corresponds to shorter PFS.

analyses.result_summary: Monotonic decrease: feature_063=0 mean PFS = 5.64 months (n=17592), level 1 = 4.44 (n=24971), level 2 = 3.29 (n=7437). One-way ANOVA F=2860.8, p<1e-300; linear trend r=-0.32, p<1e-300. Each level reduces PFS by ~1.17 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The binary feature feature_056 (prevalence 30%) is associated with substantially shorter pfs_months: patients with feature_056=1 have lower PFS than those with feature_056=0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 3.62 months when feature_056=1 (n=14969) vs 5.15 when =0 (n=35031). Welch t-test difference = -1.54 months, p < 1e-300. Largest single negative binary effect on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The binary feature feature_042 (prevalence 35%) is associated with longer pfs_months: patients with feature_042=1 have higher PFS than those with feature_042=0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 5.41 months when feature_042=1 (n=17428) vs 4.31 when =0 (n=32572). Welch t-test difference = +1.10 months, p < 1e-300. Largest single positive binary effect on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The binary feature feature_048 (prevalence 10%) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
The binary feature feature_015 (prevalence 35%) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
The binary feature feature_111 (prevalence 70%) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: feature_048=1 mean PFS = 3.75 (n=5090) vs =0 mean 4.80 (n=44910). Difference = -1.05 months, Welch p = 1.45e-178.
feature_015=1 mean PFS = 4.33 (n=17365) vs =0 mean 4.89 (n=32635). Difference = -0.56 months, Welch p = 6.07e-144.
feature_111=1 mean PFS = 4.86 (n=34886) vs =0 mean 4.30 (n=15114). Difference = +0.57 months, Welch p = 3.70e-143.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The binary feature feature_040 (prevalence 18%) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
The binary feature feature_039 (prevalence 64%) is associated with longer pfs_months.
The binary feature feature_034 (prevalence 37%) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: feature_040=1 mean PFS = 4.32 (n=9060) vs =0 mean 4.78 (n=40940). Diff = -0.46 months, Welch p = 6.10e-70.
feature_039=1 mean PFS = 4.83 (n=31954) vs =0 mean 4.46 (n=18046). Diff = +0.36 months, Welch p = 5.82e-59.
feature_034=1 mean PFS = 4.76 (n=18543) vs =0 mean 4.65 (n=31457). Diff = +0.11 months, Welch p = 1.04e-06.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous predictor feature_067 is negatively associated with pfs_months (higher values correspond to shorter PFS).
The continuous predictor feature_019 (range 1.5-5.5) is positively associated with pfs_months.
The continuous predictor feature_101 (range 1-100) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.116, p = 4.07e-148. Modest negative continuous predictor; OLS slope -0.077 months per unit.
Pearson r = +0.100, p = 9.39e-112. Positive continuous predictor; OLS slope +0.48 months per unit.
Pearson r = -0.091, p = 2.98e-93. Negative continuous predictor; OLS slope -0.019 months per unit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of feature_011 (race/ethnicity: white, black, hispanic, asian, other).

analyses.result_summary: Group means: white=4.679, black=4.717, hispanic=4.707, asian=4.735, other=4.762. One-way ANOVA F=0.85, p=0.494. NOT significant: no detectable raw PFS disparity by race/ethnicity. Maximum across-group spread ~0.08 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of feature_089 (insurance: medicaid, medicare, private, uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: Group means: medicaid=4.706, medicare=4.692, private=4.685, uninsured=4.733. ANOVA F=0.31, p=0.818. NOT significant: no detectable raw PFS disparity by insurance status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even though raw PFS does not differ by feature_011 (race), patients of different races may have different prevalence of high-risk biomarkers (feature_056, feature_048) or favorable biomarkers (feature_042, feature_111), reflecting allocation differences.

analyses.result_summary: All 8 top binary features showed no race-based prevalence differences: feature_056 chi2 p=0.71, feature_042 p=0.87, feature_048 p=0.996, feature_015 p=0.21, feature_111 p=0.64, feature_040 p=0.49, feature_039 p=0.89, feature_034 p=0.21. Prevalence within 0.04 across racial groups for every feature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients of different insurance categories (feature_089) may differ in prevalence of major prognostic biomarkers, even though raw PFS is similar.

analyses.result_summary: All 8 top binary features showed no insurance-based prevalence differences: feature_056 p=0.40, feature_042 p=0.97, feature_048 p=0.18, feature_015 p=0.44, feature_111 p=0.53, feature_040 p=0.67, feature_039 p=0.69, feature_034 p=0.96. No detectable allocation bias.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for feature_080, feature_063, and the top binary biomarkers, race (feature_011) remains a significant predictor of pfs_months (i.e., there is a residual ethnicity-based disparity).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_067 + feature_019 + feature_101 + feature_063 + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_048 + feature_015 + feature_111 + feature_040 + feature_039 + feature_034 + C(feature_011)
analyses.result_summary: Race coefficients in adjusted model: black=-0.027 (p=0.29), hispanic=-0.012 (p=0.61), other=-0.012 (p=0.78), white=-0.019 (p=0.38) — all non-significant, all <0.03 month magnitude. No residual race-based disparity in PFS after adjusting for clinical features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the same clinical predictors, insurance (feature_089) remains a significant predictor of pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Insurance coefficients in adjusted model: medicare=+0.007 (p=0.67), private=+0.000 (p=0.98), uninsured=+0.015 (p=0.60). All non-significant. No residual insurance-based disparity in PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_063 on pfs_months grows in magnitude with higher feature_080 (i.e., feature_063 and feature_080 interact).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.0057 per unit, t=-5.43, p=5.6e-08. The magnitude of the per-level decrement from feature_063 grows by ~0.006 months for each additional unit of feature_080 (small but highly significant). Stratified means confirm: feature_063 effect ~1.2 months in lowest feature_080 quintile, ~1.6 months in highest quintile.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042 and feature_111 interact synergistically: the combined positive effect on pfs_months exceeds the sum of their individual effects.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_042 + feature_111 + feature_042:feature_111 (+ covariates)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +1.578 months, p < 1e-300. feature_042 effect = +0.36 in feature_111=0 patients, but +1.94 in feature_111=1 patients. Strong positive synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042 and feature_015 interact antagonistically: the positive effect of feature_042 is attenuated (or reversed) when feature_015=1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -1.670 months, p < 1e-300. The benefit of feature_042 on PFS is roughly eliminated in feature_015=1 patients (positive effect ~+1.93 months in feature_015=0 patients shrinks to ~+0.26 in feature_015=1 patients). Strong antagonism.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 and feature_111 interact: the negative effect of feature_015 is more pronounced when feature_111=1.
feature_042 and feature_040 interact antagonistically (the favorable effect of feature_042 is attenuated when feature_040=1).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_015 + feature_111 + feature_015:feature_111 + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.842, p = 4.8e-171. The negative effect of feature_015 is amplified in feature_111=1 patients. Significant antagonism between the favorable feature_111 and feature_015.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_042 + feature_040 + feature_042:feature_040 + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -1.378, p < 1e-300. The favorable feature_042 effect is largely lost when feature_040=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 has independent prognostic value for pfs_months after adjusting for feature_111 (i.e., it is not just a duplicate of feature_111).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_080+feature_063+feature_111+feature_039 (and stratified t-tests by feature_111)
analyses.result_summary: feature_111 and feature_039 are highly correlated (phi=0.669, chi-square p<1e-300; feature_039=1 within feature_111=1 in 85%). Within feature_111=1: feature_039 effect = +0.03 (p=0.47). Within feature_111=0: feature_039 effect = -0.07 (p=0.13). In multivariable OLS adjusting for both, feature_039 coefficient = +0.014, p=0.32 — NOT significant. feature_039's univariate effect is fully explained by overlap with feature_111.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_034 has independent prognostic value for pfs_months after adjusting for feature_040 and other clinical predictors.

analyses.code: Crosstab feature_034 vs feature_040; multivariable OLS
analyses.result_summary: feature_034 and feature_040 are mutually exclusive (feature_040=1 implies feature_034=0; phi=0.36). feature_034=1 occurs only when feature_040=0; within feature_040=0 patients, feature_034 effect = -0.02 months (p=0.42). In full multivariable model adjusting for feature_080, feature_063, and other binaries, feature_034 coefficient = +0.002, p=0.88. Univariate signal is driven by collinearity with feature_040.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_055 (binary, prevalence 3%) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_077 (binary, prevalence 35%) is associated with longer pfs_months in univariate analysis.

analyses.result_summary: feature_055=1 mean PFS = 4.55 (n=1501) vs =0 mean 4.70 (n=48499). Diff = -0.14 months, Welch p = 0.024. Modest, borderline-significant negative effect.

Univariate: feature_077=1 mean PFS = 4.74 (n=17601) vs =0 mean 4.67 (n=32399). Diff = +0.07 months, p=0.005 (univariate). After adjusting for feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_042: coefficient = +0.007, p = 0.58. The univariate signal is not robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When all 124 features are entered into a multivariable OLS model, only a small subset (a dozen or so) retain independent significance at p<0.001 — the remaining variables are statistically indistinguishable from noise.

analyses.code: OLS on pfs_months with all 125 numeric features
analyses.result_summary: Of 125 candidate predictors, 12 retained p<0.001 in the joint model: feature_080 (+0.168), feature_063 (-1.171), feature_056 (-1.550), feature_048 (-0.982), feature_015 (-0.582), feature_101 (-0.019), feature_019 (+0.475), feature_067 (-0.077), feature_042 (+1.101), feature_111 (+0.532), feature_040 (-0.471), and feature_053 (-0.0005). R^2=0.793. The remaining ~110 features are effectively noise.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 distribution differs across race categories (feature_011), which could create indirect demographic disparities even though direct race effects are null.
feature_080 distribution differs across insurance categories (feature_089).

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_080 by race: white=64.94, black=65.11, hispanic=65.00, asian=65.07, other=65.38. ANOVA F=0.81, p=0.52. No across-race difference in feature_080.
Mean feature_080 by insurance: medicaid=64.96, medicare=64.97, private=65.01, uninsured=65.05. Range <0.1 across groups. No meaningful difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Distribution of stage-like feature_063 differs across race (feature_011) categories, which could indirectly drive PFS disparities.

analyses.result_summary: feature_063 distribution by race (level 0/1/2 fractions): white 35.1/50.1/14.9, black 35.0/50.2/14.8, hispanic 35.2/49.9/14.9, asian 36.5/48.4/15.1, other 35.5/49.6/14.9. Differences across racial groups <2 percentage points; chi-square non-significant. No race-based stage allocation difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_056 effect on pfs_months is approximately constant across feature_063 (stage) levels, i.e., no feature_056 x feature_063 interaction.
feature_042 effect on pfs_months is approximately constant across feature_063 levels.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of feature_056 within feature_063 levels
analyses.result_summary: feature_056 effect: stage 0 = -1.60, stage 1 = -1.51, stage 2 = -1.48 months. Effect is large and similar across all stages (each stratum p<1e-150). Effect is essentially additive (no clinically important interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of feature_042 within feature_063 levels
analyses.result_summary: feature_042 effect: stage 0 = +1.12, stage 1 = +1.09, stage 2 = +1.15 months. Effect is large and similar across all stages (each stratum p<1e-75). Approximately additive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Feature_080 has an additive (linear) effect on pfs_months without strong nonlinearity over its full range.
Within the strongest stratum of high-risk biomarkers (feature_056=1, feature_048=1), pfs_months is severely reduced even with favorable predictors (feature_042=1).

analyses.code: Quintile means of pfs_months across feature_080 quintiles
analyses.result_summary: Quintile means (Q1 to Q5): 2.28, 3.71, 4.67, 5.64, 7.21 months. Approximately linear (~1.2 months per quintile step). No evidence of dramatic threshold or saturation.

analyses.code: Mean PFS in subgroup feature_056=1 & feature_048=1 with vs without feature_042=1
analyses.result_summary: In the high-risk subgroup (feature_056=1 AND feature_048=1, n=1528): mean PFS = 2.65 months overall; with feature_042=1 mean = 3.31 (n=534), without feature_042 mean = 2.30 (n=994); diff = +1.01 months, Welch t-test p = 1.47e-15. Even in this high-risk stratum, feature_042 confers a ~1 month PFS benefit, consistent with feature_042's positive effect being preserved across risk strata.
